title: NOC 11200 – Human Resources Professionals | GTR Immigration
description: Human Resources Professionals under NOC 11200 play a critical role in workforce planning, labour relations, compensation strategy, and organizational development in Canada. With strong post-pandemic hiring trends across both private and public sectors, this occupation offers excellent prospects for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) applicants.

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree
classification.minor_group: 1120 – Human resources and business service professionals

duties: Professionals in this occupation may perform some or all of the following:
Design and implement HR and labour-relations strategies, policies, and programs
Advise management and employees on compensation, benefits, and collective agreements
Negotiate labour contracts and mediate workplace disputes
Develop job classifications, salary scales, and performance frameworks
Administer staffing, training, career development, equity, and wellness programs
Maintain HR information systems and workforce records
Evaluate workplace health-and-safety practices and recommend improvements
employment_requirements: University degree or college diploma in human resources, business administration, industrial relations, commerce, psychology, or a related field
Completion of professional HR development programs may be required
Some employers prefer the Certified Human Resources Professional (CHRP) designation
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to create 48,600 new positions for Human Resources Professionals, while approximately 46,100 job seekers may enter the field. For applicants from India and other countries, this presents promising immigration opportunities with proper planning and credential alignment.
labour_market_demand: Human Resources Professionals are consistently sought after by corporations, government agencies, unions, and consulting firms nationwide. Organizations increasingly rely on HR specialists to manage talent pipelines, workplace compliance, and employee wellbeing.
Strong immigration prospects for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $44,200 to $109,100, depending on region, seniority, and specialization.
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
